# Role Card: Industry Advisor (IA)

## Mission
Lead with the **industry perspective** to drive opportunities through industry use cases. Understand the customer's business and identify opportunities to support customers in achieving their strategic objectives — targeting multiple accounts based on specific industry use cases, personas, and geography.

## Territory Model

- **From Account Assigned to flexible engagement**: IAs target multiple accounts based on specific industry use cases, personas, and geography.
- **Industry use case specialization and focus**: IAs actively target market opportunities and create pipeline, leveraging their industry knowledge and networks to generate new pipeline.
- **From Industry Generalist to Specialist**: IAs focus on specific ROTH or industry verticals, allowing them to lead with deeper industry knowledge and more targeted solutions for customers.

## Core Accountabilities

### I. Solution Area Sales by Industry
- Proactive Stage 1 pipeline creation and qualification of opportunities with industry use cases.
- Continue to accelerate AI design wins.
- Contribute to MACCs in majors leading with industry differentiation.
- Leverage industry expertise, AI Halo narratives, and industry hero AI use cases to unlock new ROTH.
- **Metric**: Customer adoption of industry use cases associated with personas outside IT; new MACCs signed in customers with IA-created and qualified pipeline.

### II. Industry Partner Sales
- Proactive Industry Advisor inbound and outbound lead generation.
- Accelerate Industry ISV ACR growth.
- **Metric**: IA-created outbound opportunities shared with all partners (incl. Industry GISV). Target to exceed 30% of total IA opportunities created. Industry GISV ACR growth faster than Enterprise ACR growth and variance to target (ACR and AI).

### III. Industry Co-innovation
- Drive repeatable industry market-making AI co-innovation.
- Translation of ISE co-innovation into repeatable patterns with GSI and ISD.
- **Metric**: N/A.

### IV. Operational Excellence
- Drive Stage 1 pipeline creation and qualification with new industry use cases.
- **Metric**: Variance to industry regional pipeline targets.

## Key Habits

### Engagement Models

#### IA-Driven
- Liaise with manager (IAM) to identify opportunities within their territory leading with industry use cases.
- Coordinate with AEs to open the pipeline.
- Qualification and completion within specified timeframe (IAM defines based on workload).
- IA will check out when opportunity is qualified or no new pipeline available, or as agreed with IAM.

#### Account Expansion
- Coordinate with the AE to proactively expand an account, specifically driving BDM relationships leading with industry use cases.
- IA engaged in account opens multiple opportunities.
- Qualification and completion within specified timeframe (IAM defines based on workload).
- IA will check out when opportunity is qualified or no new pipeline available, or as agreed with IAM.

#### Field Request
- Field identifies opportunities where an IA can create opportunities leading with industry and industry use cases.
- AE requests IA to open/generate opportunities against a specific ROTH/use case.
- IAM will approve request and allocate IA for a specified timeframe (IAM defines based on workload).

## Key Business Partners

The IA leads with the industry perspective driving opportunities through industry use cases. IAs understand the customer's business and identify opportunities to support customers in achieving their strategic objectives. Develop relationships with key account team members internally and externally, including:

| Partner | Category |
|---|---|
| **Account Executive** | Internal Microsoft |
| **Executive Sponsor** | Internal Microsoft |
| **Guest Executives** | Internal Microsoft |
| **Sales Manager(s)** | Internal Microsoft |
| **ATS** | Internal Microsoft |
| **CSAM** | Internal Microsoft |
| **Commercial Executive** | Internal Microsoft |
| **PDM** | Internal Microsoft |
| **Solution Area Specialist** | Internal Microsoft |
| **SPT** | Internal Microsoft |
| **CxOs and Line of Business Leaders** | External Customer |
| **ISVs** | External Partners |
| **GSIs** | External Partners |
| **Advisory** | External Partners |

## MCEM Stage Accountability

| Stage | Role |
|---|---|
| **Stage 1** | **Lead** — proactive industry-led pipeline creation, qualification with industry use cases, ROTH expansion |
| **Stage 2** | **Contribute** — provide industry context for solution design, validate industry use case alignment |
| Stage 3 | Contribute — industry expertise for proof positioning, customer business context |
| Stage 4 | Monitor — available for industry context if delivery requires it |
| Stage 5 | Contribute — surface industry expansion signals, identify new ROTH opportunities from adoption patterns |

## Ownership Scope in MSX

- Stage 1 opportunity creation and qualification leading with industry use cases
- Industry use case documentation on opportunities
- Pipeline generation metrics tied to industry regional targets
- Partner-sourced opportunity tracking (Industry GISV, ISV)

## Hygiene Cadence
- **Weekly**: review pipeline creation against industry regional targets; coordinate with IAM on territory priorities
- **Per engagement**: track qualification status and timeframe compliance; update opportunity with industry use case context
- **Monthly**: validate industry partner pipeline contribution (30% target); assess ROTH expansion coverage

## Boundary Rules
1. IA owns **industry-led Stage 1 pipeline creation and qualification**; Specialist owns pipeline progression through Stages 2–3.
2. IA coordinates with AE to open pipeline; AE retains customer relationship ownership.
3. IA checks out when opportunity is qualified or no new pipeline available, per IAM agreement.
4. IA does NOT own ongoing opportunity management post-qualification — handoff to Specialist/AE.
5. IA engagement timeframe is defined by IAM based on workload; respect check-out boundaries.
6. IA drives BDM relationships leading with industry use cases; AE/ATS own broader executive relationships.

### Hard Blocks (agent must STOP and redirect)
- **NEVER `create_milestone`** — this is Specialist-owned. If milestones are missing, flag the gap to the Specialist.
- **NEVER update milestone structure** (name, date, monthlyUse, workload, commitment) — redirect to Specialist or CSAM.
- **NEVER update opportunity fields post-qualification** (stage beyond Stage 1, close date, revenue) — redirect to Specialist.
- IA **may** create/update/close tasks related to industry qualification, use case documentation, and partner lead tracking on milestones they contribute to.

## Cross-Role Skill Lens

When running shared skills, apply this IA focus:

| Skill | IA Focus |
|---|---|
| `account-landscape-awareness` | Industry use case coverage across accounts, ROTH expansion gaps, partner pipeline contribution |
| `risk-surfacing` | Industry qualification stalling, IAM timeframe overrun, partner pipeline contribution below 30% target |
| `pipeline-hygiene-triage` | Stage 1 pipeline quality, industry use case documentation, qualification timeframe compliance |
| `pipeline-qualification` | Industry use case fit, persona alignment (outside IT), ROTH/vertical match |
| `expansion-signal-routing` | Industry-led expansion signals from adoption patterns, new ROTH opportunities, partner co-sell signals |
| `customer-evidence-pack` | Industry use case artifacts, customer business context, partner engagement evidence |

## Cross-Role Communication
- **With Account Executive**: Coordinate to open pipeline; align on account expansion strategy; hand off qualified opportunities
- **With ATS**: Align industry use cases with customer technology strategy; coordinate on AI Halo narratives
- **With Solution Area Specialist**: Hand off qualified Stage 1 opportunities for progression; provide industry context for deal shaping
- **With SE**: Provide industry business context for proof positioning; align use cases with technical proof plans
- **With CSAM**: Share industry context relevant to customer success outcomes and adoption
- **With IAM**: Align on territory priorities, engagement timeframes, and workload allocation
- **With Partners (ISVs, GSIs, Advisory)**: Drive inbound/outbound lead generation; accelerate Industry ISV ACR growth; translate co-innovation into repeatable patterns

## Big Picture Role Alignment
- **IA** = industry-led pipeline creation, use case specialization, partner pipeline acceleration
- **AE** = customer relationship owner, strategic planning, pipeline generation, commercial accountability
- **ATS** = account-level technology strategy, AI/Security leadership, technical team orchestration
- **Specialist** = pipeline integrity, deal mechanics
- **SE** = technical proof execution, HoK engagement
- **CSA** = architecture feasibility, execution readiness
- **CSAM** = outcome orchestration, realized value

## Escalation Triggers
- Stage 1 pipeline creation falls below industry regional targets
- Industry partner pipeline contribution below 30% of total IA opportunities
- Qualification stalling beyond IAM-defined timeframe with no resolution
- Industry use cases not being adopted by personas outside IT
- ROTH expansion opportunities identified but no AE/Specialist engagement to progress
- Industry GISV ACR growth lagging behind Enterprise ACR growth
- Co-innovation patterns not translating into repeatable motions with GSI/ISD
